slides.title: The Power of Dynamics 📈
slides.content:
Standard arrays `String[] names = new String[5];` are strict. Once you create them with a size of 5, they can never grow to 6, and they can never shrink to 4.

An **ArrayList** is dynamic. It automatically resizes itself in memory as you add and remove items.

slides.title: CRUD Operations 🛠️
slides.content:
ArrayLists come with tons of built-in methods. Let's look at the core features:
Add, Get, Set (Update), and Remove.

quizData.question: If you need to store exactly 10 high scores and that number will NEVER change, which is more memory efficient?

options.text: A standard Array (`int[] scores = new int[10];`)

quizData.explanation: ArrayLists use more memory behind the scenes to allow for dynamic resizing. If you know the size is fixed forever, standard arrays are faster and lighter.

slides.title: Size vs Length 📏
slides.content:
One key difference when looping:

For a standard Array, you check its length: `myArray.length` (No parentheses).

For an ArrayList, it is an Object, so you call its method: `myList.size()` (With parentheses).
